removed: The system shall allow the IFA to monitor the budget balance and ensure proper financial management.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget.
reason: Both describe IFA's financial oversight, but [0] is broader team budget management while [1] focuses on monitoring; overlap in financial oversight justifies medium-confidence duplication.

removed: The system shall allow the specification of budget policies.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the IFA to audit team budgets and set regulatory policies.
reason: Specifying budget policies (4) is part of the IFA's audit and policy-setting function described in (3).

removed: The system shall allow the referee to use a mobile phone to record game events in real time.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the referee to insert game events in real time.
reason: Using a mobile phone to record events (7) is a specific method of the real-time game event insertion already described in (6).

removed: The system shall allow all users to view the complete aggregated data.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the aggregated data to be accessible to all users.
reason: Allowing all users to view aggregated data (9) is equivalent to making aggregated data accessible to all users (13).

removed: The system shall allow guests to view aggregated final data.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow unregistered users to access aggregated season data such as number of red cards and goals per team.
reason: Guests viewing final data (10) and unregistered users accessing season data (12) describe similar access for non-registered users; slight difference in scope but semantically overlapping.

removed: The system shall allow team managers and team administrators to access financial data for offline analysis.
duplicate_of: The system shall make financial data accessible to all members within the IFA and the finance department.
reason: Team access to financial data (14) is a subset of IFA and finance department access (15); partial overlap with different scope, but core behaviour of financial data access is duplicated.

removed: The system shall allow the insertion of budget transactions and items only by the respective teams.
duplicate_of: The system shall restrict budget transaction entry to individual teams, with each team only able to access and modify their own data.
reason: Allowing only respective teams to insert budget transactions (17) is a restatement of restricting transaction access to individual teams (16).

removed: The system shall allow each team to access only their own data.
duplicate_of: The system shall restrict budget transaction entry to individual teams, with each team only able to access and modify their own data.
reason: Allowing each team to access only their own data (18) directly duplicates the restriction described in (16).

removed: The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
reason: Both [26] and [27] state the system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule; exact duplication.

removed: The system shall allow fans to register for notifications related to specific games.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow users to subscribe to each type of information mentioned and receive notifications accordingly.
reason: Registering for game notifications (32) is a specific case of subscribing to information types (34); partial functional overlap.

removed: The system shall send a notification to users when a message is posted by a player such as Messi.
duplicate_of: The system shall send push email notifications to fans when their registered favorite player posts a message.
reason: Notifying users when a player posts a message (33) is a specific instance of the push notification system described in (31).

removed: The system shall send a notification to users registered for a game when a player replacement is reported by the referee after the game has started.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow users to subscribe to each type of information mentioned and receive notifications accordingly.
reason: Sending notifications for player replacements (35) is a specific case of the general subscription and notification system in (34).

removed: The system shall provide a web-based application for teams and IFA administrators to use.
duplicate_of: The system shall include all functionality within the application or website.
reason: Providing a web-based application (37) is part of including all functionality in the application or website (36).

removed: The system shall provide mobile applications for referees and fans.
duplicate_of: The system shall include all functionality within the application or website.
reason: Providing mobile apps (38) supports the overall goal of including all functionality in the system (36), but is a platform-specific implementation.

removed: The system shall provide a unified system with the same goal as the current separate systems.
duplicate_of: The system shall provide different interfaces within the same system for various user roles or functions.
reason: Providing a unified system (40) aligns with providing different interfaces for roles (39); both address system integration and role-based access.

removed: The system shall ensure that referees and official channels are the sole sources of game data within the system.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow only referees to update game data.
reason: Ensuring referees and official channels are the sole sources of game data (43) reinforces the requirement that only referees can update game data (41).

removed: The system shall enable the referee to receive responses immediately.
duplicate_of: The system shall provide a response to the referee within a reasonable time frame.
reason: Requiring immediate response to referees (49) is a stronger form of providing a response within a reasonable time frame (45); same core behaviour.

removed: The system shall provide real-time updates from the referee to the main console.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the referee to insert game events in real time.
reason: Providing real-time updates from referees (50) is part of inserting game events in real time (6).

removed: The system shall enable referees to enter match events into the application in real time to ensure accountability and compliance auditing.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow referees to enter match events into the application in real time during the match.
reason: Enabling referees to enter match events in real time (74) is identical to allowing referees to enter events during the match (77).

removed: The system shall automatically send a notification to fans who have registered for a specific game if the date or time of that game is changed.
duplicate_of: The system shall automatically notify any fan who has registered to follow a specific game if the schedule for that game is changed.
reason: Automatically notifying fans of game schedule changes (78) and notifying registered fans of schedule changes (79) describe the same system behaviour.

removed: The system shall implement reliable data backup mechanisms to prevent loss of financial and scheduling records.
duplicate_of: The system shall perform daily backups of all data.
reason: Implementing reliable backup mechanisms (83) is a general form of performing daily backups (82); same intent with different phrasing.

removed: The system shall allow fans to filter upcoming fixtures by team or by date.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow fans to filter upcoming fixtures by team or by date.
reason: Allowing fans to filter fixtures by team or date is repeated verbatim in both (85) and (87).

removed: The system shall automatically generate the full match schedule for each league at the start of every season.
duplicate_of: The system shall automatically generate the season schedule for each league at the start of the season.
reason: Automatically generating the full match schedule (92) is the same as generating the season schedule (91).

removed: The system shall consider home and away rotation, minimum rest periods between fixtures, and permitted match days when generating the league schedule.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow the IFA to set the rules governing how games are scheduled.
reason: Considering scheduling constraints (94) supports the ability to set scheduling rules (93); closely related but not fully duplicative, though overlapping in intent.

removed: The system shall allow a new team to be registered only after the team owner has submitted the required official documents to the IFA and the IFA has approved the application.
duplicate_of: The system shall allow team managers or owners to submit a new team registration with valid documents, which can be approved by the IFA.
reason: Requiring document submission and IFA approval for team registration (99) duplicates the process described in (65).

removed: The system shall send a notification to fans who follow a specific player when that player scores a goal during a match.
duplicate_of: The system shall send push email notifications to fans when their registered favorite player posts a message.
reason: Sending goal notifications to fans (103) is a specific case of the push/email notification system for player messages (31).

removed: The system shall allow a fan to follow a specific football club so that they receive all notifications related to that club's matches and activities.
duplicate_of: The system shall send a notification to fans who follow a specific player when that player scores a goal during a match.
reason: Allowing fans to follow a club (104) enables receiving notifications about that club, overlapping with goal and game notifications (103).

removed: The system shall preserve the final league table and all match results for every completed season.
duplicate_of: The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
reason: Preserving final league tables and match results (106) is part of retaining past scheduling archives (66).

removed: The system shall display the name of the assigned referee for each upcoming match.
duplicate_of: The system shall display the venue and kick-off time for every scheduled match.
reason: Displaying referee name (112) and displaying venue/kick-off time (111) are distinct pieces of match scheduling info; low-confidence duplication due to shared context but different data.
